claim.summary: Delegated queue decomposed and completed a project-family shared-repo task through two worker jobs plus one dependency-gated integrator under an isolated mock runtime.

focused_tests.command: pytest -q tests/test_job_queue.py -k "project_parallel_release_task_decomposes or decomposes_parallel_workers or defers_integrator_until_worker_dependencies_complete or stale_lease_and_resumes_orphaned or promotable_cli_excludes"

patched_gap.issue: Multi-job decomposition had no built-in non-repo_sandbox task surface; the first project task contract also omitted worker report artifacts from expected_changed_paths.
patched_gap.fix: Added project_parallel_release_task with two explicit project-family worker specs and included each worker report artifact in its verifier expected_changed_paths.
patched_gap.verification: The fresh isolated drain completed both project workers and the project integrator with outcome=success and zero active leases.

open_limits: This is project-family multi-job substrate evidence, not an A5 promotion claim.
The isolated CLI run used provider=mock and disabled command containment, so it is not production-equivalent runtime evidence.
Shared-repo reports still show hidden_side_effect_risk=true because git runtime artifacts are broad; this packet proves multi-job handoff breadth, not clean side-effect breadth.
Multi-job breadth for integration, repository, and repo_chore remains open.
